questions.text: When you discover a new podcast you're into, how do you find it — do people recommend shows to you, do you actively search for topics you're curious about, or do you just let algorithms guide you? And once you find one, do you binge entire seasons or listen episode-by-episode as they drop?

When you're building a friendship with someone new — what actually makes you decide to invest more time and energy into that person? Is there a specific quality, conversation topic, or vibe that signals to you 'this person is going to be in my inner circle'?

When you need to buy something — let's say a gadget, some household item, or something for your place — do you mostly shop online or do you prefer going to physical stores? And how do you feel about buying used or secondhand versus new, especially when you're in a new place and don't know the local marketplaces?

What's your actual approach to managing money day-to-day — are you someone who budgets closely (tracking every expense), do you have a rough mental sense of your spending, or do you mostly just wing it? And do you have specific financial goals you're actively working toward right now?

When you're learning something new for fun — not work-related — what's your preferred way to learn? Do you dive into books, watch videos, take online courses, learn by doing, or talk to people who know more than you?
